extraction.one_line_summary: SAGAN Peter won a reduced sprint finish after late catch of breakaway at 22km to go, using team control throughout climbs to preserve sprint opportunity.
tactical_patterns: SAGAN Peter deliberately sat fifth wheel before final kilometer, waited for MATTHEWS Michael leadout to commit before attacking barriers side.
Peloton caught breakaway with 22km remaining rather than final climb summit, suggesting GC teams wanted controlled finish not selection.
ALAPHILIPPE Julian positioned for stage bonuses despite wearing yellow, revealing Deceuninck prioritized time gaps over defensive riding early tour.
Green jersey sprint required positioning through Category 2 climbs, eliminating pure sprinters like VIVIANI Elia from contention despite team support.

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: Showed climbing durability sufficient to contest reduced sprints after multiple Cat 2 ascents, tactical patience waiting fifth wheel.
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: neutral: Leadout reached final kilometer in strong position but lacked finishing speed against SAGAN, suggesting form limits not tactics.
rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: positive: European champion's late acceleration nearly caught SAGAN, indicating top-end speed competitive in reduced bunch sprints.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Yellow jersey attempted stage finish sprint despite defensive obligations, confidence signal about form depth and team ambition.
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: neutral: Polka dot jersey allowed to collect KOM points by breakaway cooperation, standard professional courtesy limiting racing aggression early.
rider_signals.CLARKE Simon: neutral: EF captain took intermediate sprint in breakaway through team consensus, showing established hierarchy even in doomed moves.
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: World champion sat far back on penultimate climb showing no GC ambition, treating stage as transition day.
